recaps.text: Bilbo Baggins, a home-loving hobbit, is talked into joining Gandalf and thirteen dwarves led by Thorin Oakenshield on a journey to reclaim the dwarves' treasure from the dragon Smaug. Early on the party is nearly eaten by trolls, rests at Rivendell, and is captured by goblins in the misty mountains. Escaping in the dark, Bilbo is separated from the others and finds a plain gold ring, then meets the creature Gollum and wins a riddle-game to survive - slipping away past Gollum using the ring, which makes its wearer invisible. Reunited with the dwarves, the company is chased up trees by wolves and goblins and rescued by great eagles.

Carried to safety, the company is sheltered by Beorn, a man who can become a bear, then enters the dark forest of Mirkwood alone after Gandalf leaves them on other business. They nearly starve, are trapped by giant spiders whom Bilbo fights off with his ring and small sword, and are then captured by the wood-elves. Bilbo, invisible, engineers their escape by hiding the dwarves in empty barrels floated downriver to Lake-town, whose people welcome the returning heir of Thorin. At last the travellers reach the Lonely Mountain and, using the map's moon-letter clue, find the hidden door in its side.

Bilbo creeps down the secret passage and, invisible, speaks with the dragon Smaug, flattering him while noticing a bare patch in his armored underside. Enraged and suspicious, Smaug flies out to destroy Lake-town in revenge. But a thrush carries word of the weak spot to Bard the archer, who looses a black arrow that finds the gap and kills the dragon. The Lonely Mountain and its vast treasure now lie open and unguarded.

With Smaug dead, men of the lake and wood-elves march on the mountain seeking a share of the hoard, but Thorin, gripped by greed, refuses them and summons other dwarves to defend it. As battle looms, an army of goblins and wolves descends, forcing men, elves, and dwarves to fight side by side in the Battle of Five Armies. Thorin is mortally wounded but makes peace with Bilbo before he dies. Given a modest share of the treasure, Bilbo travels home with Gandalf to find his belongings being auctioned off, and settles back at Bag End - changed for good, and keeping the magic ring quietly to himself.
